items.title: Why the U.S. and Iran can't reach a peace deal | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains what's keeping the U.S. and Iran from reaching a peace agreement following six weeks of ongoing war and a recent marathon negotiating session in Pakistan. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images

items.title: Russian news agency fined for posting review of Heated Rivalry
items.summary_auto: A Russian court has fined a local news agency and one of its directors thousands of dollars for a review it posted on the smash hit series Heated Rivalry, which the Russian government considers LGBTQ+ propaganda.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | How the Artemis II crew made out-of-this-world images
items.summary_auto: The Artemis II astronauts made many mind-blowing images during their historic lunar mission earlier this month. They have two NASA trainers to thank for equipping them with tried-and-true digital SLR cameras and sharing their photo skills.

items.title: Raffle winner surprised — even skeptical — upon landing $1M Picasso
items.summary_auto: A Parisian art enthusiast could not believe his luck when he found out Tuesday he'd won a Pablo Picasso painting worth more than $1 million in a raffle.

items.title: 2 Royal Canadian Navy sailors charged in fatal Halifax harbour capsizing
items.summary_auto: Petty Officer 2nd Class Gregory Applin of Shoal Cove West, N.L., died after he was thrown into frigid water when an inflatable boat hit an unlit buoy.

items.title: Originally promised to be free, Toronto now wants to charge for FIFA Fan Fest
items.summary_auto: Once promised as a free way for Torontonians and visitors to take part in one of the world’s largest sporting events, the city is now looking to charge for tickets to the 2026 FIFA World Cup fan festival.

items.title: Are you paying more than your neighbour? It could be 'surveillance pricing'
items.summary_auto: The federal NDP is introducing a motion on Wednesday to ban so-called surveillance pricing, where personal data is used to offer individual customers or groups of people different prices than others. While finding specific examples is difficult, critics argue it's a serious problem.

items.title: Canadian oil and gas losing competitive edge due to industrial carbon price: industry leaders
items.summary_auto: Leaders in Canada's oil and gas sector say the industrial carbon levy would erode the country's competitive edge at a time when the world is clamouring for a reliable energy supply.

items.title: 'We were a normal family … but then everything changed': Refugees recount brutal toll of Sudan's civil war
items.summary_auto: Three years on from the outbreak of war in Sudan in April 2023, the country is in the grip of what the United Nations calls the world’s worst displacement crisis, with more than 11 million people forced from their homes.

items.title: Canada pledges $120M in humanitarian assistance as civil war in Sudan enters 4th year
items.summary_auto: Canada has announced more than $120 million in humanitarian and development assistance to help people in Sudan and its neighbours as the civil war in that country marks its third anniversary.

items.title: Ye postpones show in France amid possible ban
items.summary_auto: The rapper formerly known as Kanye West postponed his upcoming show in the city of Marseille after French authorities said they would seek to ban the concert.

items.title: Political strain between U.S. and Britain on full display days ahead of King Charles’s American visit
items.summary_auto: British Finance Minister Rachel Reeves was not backing away from her criticism of the Iran war as "folly," even as she prepared to meet Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and other U.S. officials in Washington on Wednesday.

items.title: Reality TV star Taylor Frankie Paul will not face new domestic violence charges
items.summary_auto: Taylor Frankie Paul, a star of The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives and the recently scrubbed season of The Bachelorette, will not be charged in recent fights with her former partner, the Salt Lake County District Attorney's Office said Tuesday.

items.title: 9 dead in Turkey's 2nd school shooting this week
items.summary_auto: A student opened fire on two classrooms at a middle school in Turkey on Wednesday, killing nine people and wounding 13 others, the interior minister said, in the country's second such shooting in two days.

items.title: Children killed in Lebanon as Israeli strikes hit homes far from the war's front lines
items.summary_auto: Israel has often struck alleged Hezbollah militants or officials in their homes without warning, frequently in areas far from the front line when they are with their families, in apartment buildings surrounded by uninvolved neighbours.

items.title: Alberta sticking to its gas tax formula as Ottawa announces temporary cut
items.summary_auto: While Prime Minister Mark Carney announced federal measures to give Canadians relief at the pumps Tuesday, Alberta says its fuel tax will stay in place for now.

items.title: Christine Fréchette will become Quebec's premier today. Here's how she got here
items.summary_auto: It has been just four years since Christine Fréchette took office with the Coalition Avenir Québec. Later today, she will be sworn in as the province’s 33rd premier. Here's a look at how she got here.

items.title: Some Alberta doctors caught in hospitalist contract dispute facing disciplinary action
items.summary_auto: Doctors and hospital leaders are at loggerheads in Edmonton, with disciplinary action, legal threats and staffing confusion in the wake of expired contracts that changed how hundreds of doctors across the province are paid.

items.title: Sask. Iranians deal with fear, guilt over distance from family as war rages on
items.summary_auto: Despite hopes and optimism when the strikes began, the people of Iran are now in a worse situation than before the war, says executive director of the Saskatoon Iranian Cultural Association.

items.title: N.S. mother calls for more inclusion support after autistic son went missing from school
items.summary_auto: The boy, 5, ran away from his pre-primary group and was missing for a half-hour before he was found safely in the woods behind the school. His mother believes he waded knee-deep into a stream while he was gone.

items.title: At least 250 people missing after boat sinks in Indian Ocean
items.summary_auto: At least 250 people, including Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals, were missing after a boat capsized in the Andaman Sea recently on the way to Malaysia, according to the United Nations' refugee and migration agencies.

items.title: N.L. health minister calls new details of travel nurse agency billings ‘egregious’
items.summary_auto: Health Minister Lela Evans had some strong words about past billings by a company that provided travel nurses to the province.

items.title: Finland's president on why he believes Canada could — eventually — be part of the EU
Aging oil wells on her land are making this Alberta farmer's life miserable. She's not alone
items.summary_auto: Teresa Patry, a Vermilion, Alta., farmer and rancher, has two active oil wells operating on her land, which, according to an air quality assessment, are venting a steady stream of potentially dangerous greenhouse gases downwind from where she lives with her family and livestock. But, according to Alberta's energy regular, everything is operating as it should be.

items.title: Did Winners close some of its bathrooms? Questions swirl amid debate over toilet access
items.summary_auto: Frustrated shoppers allege the TJX-owned chains Winners, HomeSense and Marshalls have recently shut down bathrooms in some Canadian locations.

items.title: Dosa Divas stirs the pot with cooking, robot battles and a dash of family drama
items.summary_auto: Dosa Divas doesn't stray too far from its predecessor Thirsty Suitors, but it streamlines the experience while telling a story about family reconciliation that feels realistic despite the colourful, fantastical setting.

items.title: Eastern Ontario farmers brace for rising costs from U.S.-Iran war
items.summary_auto: Farmers in eastern Ontario are warning that rising fuel and fertilizer costs due to the U.S.-Iran war could soon drive up the price of locally grown food.

items.title: Armed with paintbrushes, volunteers try to preserve rare books at Gaza City's Omari library
items.summary_auto: A group of volunteers had begun sifting through the damage at the Omari library in Gaza City and trying to save whatever books they can find. They say the work is not just about the books, it's about protecting the culture and heritage of the Gaza Strip.

items.title: PWHL stars are having a cultural moment. Their salaries are not keeping up
items.summary_auto: Hockey fans may have tuned in to the 2026 Olympics excited about the long-awaited return of NHL players, but by the end it was the Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) that turned heads and  earned a growing wave  of new fans.

items.title: The tough job market isn't getting any better for young Canadians
items.summary_auto: Thousands of young people are turning to job fairs to try and stand out in-person to find a gig for this summer, but competition remains intense.

items.title: Food suppliers are adding surcharges to foot the rising cost of fuel. That could mean even pricier groceries
items.summary_auto: At least four suppliers are adding fuel surcharges for deliveries of food to grocers, according to documents obtained by CBC News. While the costs are already affecting some sticker prices, some grocers say they're hesitant to increase prices too quickly.

items.title: Next level nests: How these Alberta birds are getting home upgrades
items.summary_auto: Just in time for nesting season, volunteers with the Calgary Area NestBox Monitors Society are replacing worn-out bird boxes with newer models. We tagged along to see what's involved and why the work is so important for a variety of species.

items.title: Does Vancouver have the appetite for an MLB team? Critics doubt mayor's proposal
items.summary_auto: As Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim puts forward a motion seeking to attract a Major League Baseball (MLB) franchise to the city, critics are doubtful whether the city can put forward an attractive enough business case and sustain the franchise.

items.title: 7-Eleven sandwiches, subs and wraps recalled over Listeria concerns
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Food Inspection Agency on Tuesday ordered a recall of 7-Eleven brand sandwiches, subs and wraps due to potential contamination of Listeria monocytogenes.
